title: Automotive Focus: BlackBerry's QNX Driving into the Future
text: BlackBerry's QNX embedded operating system is driving the automotive industry into the future with record growth and strategic partnerships. QNX announced that its technology will be integrated into BMW's new Neue Klasse vehicle platform to support safety-critical systems, building on a multi-year partnership launched in 2021 to deploy SAE Level 2/2+ driver assistance systems across BMW's next-generation models. In late June 2025, QNX signed a memorandum of understanding with Vector to develop an open Foundational Vehicle Software Platform for software-defined vehicles, aiming to unify their safety OS, middleware and tool chains so OEMs and Tier-1s can share and reuse code. In April 2025, QNX teamed up with Chinese autonomous-drive leader WeRide Inc. to power its WeRide WePilot L2++ ADAS system, with QNX OS for Safety serving as the foundation. BlackBerry has recently achieved design wins with industry leaders such as Aptiv, Denso, Ford, GM, Hyundai, LG Electronics, Magna, and Volvo. Continued revenue growth in the QNX segment is being driven by new automotive design wins and expansion into robotics, industrial systems, and AI-driven applications. The company maintains a $950 million royalty backlog, providing significant visibility into future revenue.
